key_insight: Lean can be used not just to verify proofs at the end, but to provide dense, step-level reward signals during training for theorem-proving RL.
engineering_relevance: This enables more sample-efficient and reliable training of language-model provers by grounding credit assignment in symbolic type-theoretic checks instead of sparse binary rewards.
method: Parse proof attempts into Lean tactic sequences
Use Lean elaboration to label locally sound steps and identify the first failing step
Train with a GRPO-style objective using first-error propagation and first-token credit
evidence_signal: Experiments on STP-Lean and DeepSeek-Prover-V1.5 show tactic-level supervision improves performance over outcome-only baselines on benchmarks including MiniF2F and ProofNet.
watch_item: The approach depends on robust tactic parsing and Lean's feedback quality, so errors in proof state handling or reward shaping could reduce training stability.
paper_title: Process-Verified Reinforcement Learning for Theorem Proving via Lean
